mission.title: Install and Configure Knative Eventing (Knative) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Knative Eventing is an event-driven application platform for Kubernetes that enables secure event processing and discovery using CloudEvents. Installing Knative Eventing allows you to build and manage event-driven applications seamlessly on your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Add the Knative Eventing Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Knative Eventing Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add knative-eventing https://charts.knative.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Knative Eventing
steps.description:
Install Knative Eventing using Helm in the `knative-eventing` namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install knative-eventing knative-eventing/knative-eventing --namespace knative-eventing --create-namespace --version knative-v1.21.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all pods in the `knative-eventing` namespace are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-eventing
```

steps.title: Configure default channel
steps.description:
Apply the default channel configuration to set up the InMemoryChannel as the default.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: config-br-default-channel
  namespace: knative-eventing
data:
  channel-template-spec: |
    apiVersion: messaging.knative.dev/v1
    kind: InMemoryChannel
EOF
```

steps.title: Configure broker defaults
steps.description:
Apply the broker defaults configuration to set the default broker class.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: config-br-defaults
  namespace: knative-eventing
data:
  default-br-config: |
    clusterDefault:
      brokerClass: MTChannelBasedBroker
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Knative Eventing will show all pods in the `knative-eventing` namespace as running. You will also have the default channel and broker configurations applied, enabling event-driven capabilities in your Kubernetes cluster.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add knative-eventing https://charts.knative.dev
helm repo update
helm install knative-eventing knative-eventing/knative-eventing --namespace knative-eventing --create-namespace --version knative-v1.21.0

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: config-br-default-channel
  namespace: knative-eventing
data:
  channel-template-spec: |
    apiVersion: messaging.knative.dev/v1
    kind: InMemoryChannel
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: config-br-defaults
  namespace: knative-eventing
data:
  default-br-config: |
    clusterDefault:
      brokerClass: MTChannelBasedBroker
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Knative Eventing release from the `knative-eventing` namespace using Helm.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Knative Eventing.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the `knative-eventing` namespace and verify that it has been removed.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Knative Eventing installation.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository and run the upgrade command to upgrade Knative Eventing to the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check that all pods in the `knative-eventing` namespace are running successfully after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the failing pod to diagnose the issue. Use the following command to view logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Broker not receiving events
troubleshooting.description:
If the broker is not receiving events, ensure that the event source is correctly configured and that the broker is running. Check the broker status with:
```bash
kubectl get brokers --namespace knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration changes not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If configuration changes are not taking effect, verify that the ConfigMaps have been applied correctly. Check the ConfigMaps with:
```bash
kubectl get configmaps --namespace knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If a service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running and check the ingress configuration. Use the following command to check the service status:
```bash
kubectl get services --namespace knative-eventing
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
